About.Name: Jainaba Jallow
About.AboutText: I am an Electrical Engineering student at Seattle Pacific University with a strong interest in power systems, electrical infrastructure, and applied engineering design. My academic work has focused on circuit analysis, electricity and magnetism, and power systems, where I have developed a solid foundation in three-phase systems and electrical system behavior. Beyond the classroom, I enjoy working on hands-on engineering projects, including my involvement in the BAJA SAE electronics team, where I collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to design and integrate sensor-based monitoring systems. I also have experience with engineering tools such as PSCAD, AutoCAD, and Excel for system analysis and technical documentation. My long-term goal is to contribute to improving electrical infrastructure and expanding reliable access to electricity in communities where energy systems are still developing.

Experience.ExperienceText: Growing up in The Gambia, I witnessed firsthand how unreliable electricity and limited infrastructure affect households, businesses, and opportunities for development. Those experiences shaped my long-term goal of becoming an electrical engineer who can contribute to improving electrical infrastructure and expanding reliable access to energy in developing communities. I believe that through education, technical training, and collaboration with other engineers, I can help develop practical and sustainable electrical solutions that support long-term growth.
Experience.EducationText: My studies focus on developing a strong foundation in electrical systems, circuit analysis, and power engineering. Through coursework in subjects such as circuit analysis, electricity and magnetism, logic systems, and power systems, I am building the analytical and technical skills required to understand and design complex electrical systems. The program emphasizes both theoretical knowledge and practical problem-solving, preparing me to contribute to real-world engineering challenges in energy and infrastructure systems.
Experience.EmploymentText: As an IT Support Technician at Seattle Pacific University, I assist students, faculty, and staff with troubleshooting technical issues related to computers, audiovisual systems, and classroom technology. This role requires a structured approach to diagnosing problems, identifying possible causes, and implementing effective solutions. I also maintain documentation through a ticketing system to track service requests and record troubleshooting steps. This experience has strengthened my problem-solving abilities, technical communication, and organizational skills while supporting the reliable operation of campus technology systems.
Experience.SkillsText:
Technical Skills: 3-Phase Systems • Breadboard Design • Circuit Troubleshooting • Function Generator & Oscilloscope Operation • Soldering • Asset Data Organization
Computer Software / Tools: PSCAD • Excel • Jira • AutoCAD • Microsoft Office • Microsoft Entra • Multisim • MATLAB • QTSPIM • LTSpice • GitHub • CLion • Arduino IDE • Visio

Programming Languages: Python • C++ • MATLAB • Arduino • MIPS
